name: Credit.Org - Goodyear, Az Branch
description_short: Nonprofit credit and housing counselor offering free debt relief, credit building, and HUD-approved housing counseling since 1974 with certified advisors.
description_long:
Credit.org is a nonprofit financial counseling organization that has operated since 1974, delivering over $2.2 billion in homeowner support and completing more than 5.2 million counseling sessions. The organization operates a national network with a Goodyear, Arizona branch, providing free or low-cost financial guidance to consumers seeking debt relief, credit improvement, and housing stability.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of services including certified credit counseling, debt management plans, bankruptcy education, reverse mortgage counseling (both HECM and non-HECM), pre-purchase homebuying education, foreclosure prevention assistance, rental readiness counseling, and disaster financial recovery support. They also provide educational resources through FIT Academy (self-paced courses), live webinars, and interactive calculators covering budgeting, debt management, credit building, and homeownership preparation.

Credit.org distinguishes itself through HUD housing counselor certification, NFCC affiliation (implied by the nonprofit structure and service model), and a three-step process combining initial assessment, personalized plan creation, and ongoing support. The organization emphasizes bilingual services (English/Spanish), workplace financial wellness programs, and partnerships with national and community organizations. Their reverse mortgage counseling services are particularly notable, with required and optional educational tracks.

The primary limitation is that while comprehensive, the organization's free services are advisory and educational rather than debt settlement or legal bankruptcy filing. Consumers seeking immediate debt forgiveness or aggressive legal intervention would need external providers. The service quality and availability may vary by branch location, and counseling effectiveness depends on client commitment to implementing recommendations.
pros: Genuinely nonprofit with HUD-approved housing counselors and NFCC-certified advisors—no hidden sales commissions
Completely free initial counseling sessions with no pressure to purchase services
Comprehensive service menu covering debt, credit, housing, reverse mortgages, foreclosure, and disaster recovery
5.2+ million completed counseling sessions and $2.2 billion in homeowner support documented since 1974
Bilingual services in English and Spanish increasing accessibility
Free educational resources including FIT Academy courses, live webinars, budgeting calculators, and debt management tools
Specialized reverse mortgage counseling (both HECM and non-HECM options) with dedicated counselors
cons: Free counseling is educational and advisory only—does not include debt settlement, legal representation, or bankruptcy filing
Service quality and availability depend on local branch location; Goodyear branch specifics not documented on website
No pricing or service tier information provided, creating uncertainty about when paid services might be recommended
Testimonials show only positive reviews with no balanced criticism or discussion of limitations
Response time and appointment scheduling ease not documented—800 number provided but no expected wait times
best_for: Homeowners considering reverse mortgages who need required counseling and want unbiased education
Consumers with moderate debt or credit issues seeking free guidance before debt becomes critical
Renters and first-time homebuyers needing educational foundation on housing costs and mortgage readiness
Individuals recovering from financial disasters seeking structured financial recovery planning
diagnosis: Credit.org is best for homeowners needing HUD reverse mortgage counseling, renters/first-time buyers building housing readiness knowledge, and consumers with moderate debt seeking free unbiased guidance. Primary caveat: This is education and planning-focused counseling, not debt settlement or legal bankruptcy representation—consumers needing immediate debt forgiveness or court intervention must seek specialized debt relief or bankruptcy attorneys elsewhere.
services: Free initial debt and financial assessment counseling
Credit score improvement and credit report dispute counseling
HECM and non-HECM reverse mortgage counseling (required FHA counseling)
Foreclosure prevention and mortgage default assistance
Rental readiness coaching and rental assistance counseling
FIT Academy self-paced online courses (budgeting, credit, debt, homeownership)
Live interactive webinars and in-person classes
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 15150 W Park Pl, 2Nd Floor, Goodyear, AZ 85395
